question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail when the Northern Ireland Community Infrastructure Fund will open for expressions of interest.
answer_text: I will announce the details for the Northern Ireland Community Infrastructure Fund on Wednesday 24th September 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the closure of the Non-Domestic Renewable Heat Incentive Scheme.
To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) the number of people with a diagnosis of propionic acidemia; and (ii) the accessibility of carglumic acid as treatment for propionic acidemia.
answer_text: Propionic Acidaemia is a rare and inherited metabolic condition which can be treated using a specialised protein restricted diet and / or medication.  Propionic Acidaemia occurs in approximately 1 in 50,000 to 1 in 100,000 births worldwide.There are currently no adult patients with a diagnosis of Propionic Acidaemia.From a paediatric perspective, the service has confirmed that there are currently less than 5 patients with Propionic Acidaemia.  I am unable to provide the exact number of patients diagnosed with Propionic Acidaemia to reduce any risk of identification of individual patients. Carglumic acid is used for treatment where clinically appropriate, and there have not been issues accessing the drug to date.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any discussions he has had with the Minister for Communities in relation to the allocation of an industry levy, as provided for under the Betting, Gaming, Lotteries and Amusements (Amendment) Act (Northern Ireland) 2022.
answer_text: Whilst I have had no specific discussions with the Minister for Communities regarding the proposed levy for Northern Ireland, my officials continue to carry out scoping work to explore the possible impact and implications of a levy in Northern Ireland.Engagement between my officials and DfC officials is ongoing through the Cross Departmental Working Group on Preventing and Treating Gambling Related Harm arrangements and this issue will be further considered through this mechanism.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail an update on the progress of the hygiene pod being installed in St Killian's College, Garron Tower to enable a special needs child to start their new school after the holidays, as committed to by the Department in April 2025.
answer_text: My officials have been working closely with the school and the Education Authority (EA) to ensure that the hygiene pod will be installed at St Killian’s in advance of the new academic year. The intention to award letter issued to the successful contractor on 2 July 2025 and the EA is in the process of arranging a meeting with the school and the contractor to confirm the delivery programme.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail his assessment of the resources his Department has in place to implement the industry levy on gambling operators as provided for under the Betting, Gaming, Lotteries and Amusements (Amendment) Act  2022 within the 2022-2027 mandate.
answer_text: My officials are currently gathering the data required to explore how a gambling industry levy could be introduced in Northern Ireland. This exercise will provide detail of the corresponding resource requirement.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether he plans to publish the Gambling Code of Practice by the end of 2025, as reported by his officials to the Committee for Communities during an oral briefing on 26 June 2025.
answer_text: Subject to final consideration of the responses to the 2024 consultation on the initial draft Gambling Code of Practice, it remains my intention to publish Northern Ireland’s first Gambling Code of Practice as soon as practicably possible in 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether prize draws operating in the North are regulated under the Betting, Gaming, Lotteries and Amusements Order 1985 and the Betting, Gaming, Lotteries and Amusements (Amendment) Act  2022.
answer_text: Where prize draws provide a free entry route they are not captured under The Betting, Gaming, Lotteries and Amusements (NI) Order 1985 (‘the Order). 	Where no free entry route is provided they are considered an illegal lottery and are captured under Article 131 of the Order.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health (i) for his assessment of the Down Syndrome Act 2022; and (ii) whether he has any plans to introduce similar legislation.
answer_text: It is not yet clear how the Down Syndrome Act would add to the existing legislative framework for people with disabilities in Northern Ireland. Health and Social Care services for people with Down Syndrome already forms part of a wider service for people with learning disabilities, which has an established legislative framework. People with Down Syndrome would also fall within the scope of legislation for Autism and Special Educational Needs.Therefore, I have no immediate plans to replicate the Down Syndrome Act 2022 in Northern Ireland. The Chronically Sick and Disabled Persons (NI) Act 1978 remains the primary legislative framework which guides the provision of social care for people with learning disabilities. As part of delivery of the Learning Disability Service Model, my officials will consider any potential legislative changes.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the Electronic Travel Authorisation scheme.
answer_text: The ETA Scheme is consistently raised during my engagements with tourism businesses and stakeholder representatives. I share their strong concerns about its impact on our tourism product and our wider economy.  This view is also shared right across our Assembly Chamber. I continue to lobby the British Home Office for solutions and had a constructive meeting with Seema Malhotra, the Minister for Migration and Citizenship, in London on 18th June regarding the scheme.She has not ruled out an exemption and is open to receiving evidence on the impact of the scheme.So, compiling that evidence is vital and this is something I have tasked my officials to consider in collaboration with representative bodies such as the NI Tourism Alliance and the Inbound Tour Operators Association.It’s also encouraging that Minister Malhotra is visiting Belfast tomorrow to meet with the sector and hear about the impacts at first hand.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the Fuel Poverty Strategy.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on works to replace, repair and re-position (i) road signage; and (ii) street furniture, damaged as a result of recent storms.
answer_text: The initial intensive recovery phase following Storm Eowyn has been completed. However, with significant damage caused to our road infrastructure some of the repair work is complex and time consuming. While my Department continues to complete this work, as well as continue to deliver its normal service, it is likely that it will take many months to have all the damage caused fully repaired.While some capital works associated with Storm Éowyn have been undertaken, this has been achieved by my Department reprioritising workloads within existing budgets. Any localised concerns in relation to damaged road signage should be directed to the relevant divisional office.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update an any resurfacing schemes to improve the current surface of the Loughbeg Road, Toomebridge.
answer_text: Structural Maintenance works are funded through my Department’s Structural Maintenance Programme; regrettably, for some time the available investment has not kept pace with what is needed to maintain all of our infrastructure in a reasonable condition.While there are many roads that would benefit from investment, including the Loughbeg Road, Toomebridge, due to limited budget resources, resurfacing work is taken forward through the prioritisation of those deemed most in need for intervention.Details of the resurfacing schemes planned for the 2025/26 financial year will be included in my Department’s Annual Report to Council.My officials will continue to undertake regular inspections and repair defects in accordance with the Department’s Limited Service policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to outline any plans to provide a footbridge over the river Bann in Portglenone village.
answer_text: I recognise that the historic Portglenone Bridge would benefit from improved pedestrian facilities along the busy A42 Clady Road crossing the Bann and my department is giving consideration to assessing the priority of a separate pedestrian bridge.However the significant costs such an investment would require, and the current difficult financial environment, means that work must be taken forward through the prioritisation of that deemed most in need for intervention.It is unlikely that this proposal will be taken forward in the short term.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update an any resurfacing schemes to improve the current road surface of the Cushendall Road, from Cargan village to the entrance of Glenariff Forest Park.
answer_text: Structural Maintenance works are funded through my Department’s Structural Maintenance Programme; regrettably, for some time the available investment has not kept pace with what is needed to maintain all of our infrastructure in a reasonable condition.While there are many carriageways across the road network, including the A43 Glenravel Road / Glenariff Road, which would benefit from investment, due to limited budget resources, structural maintenance work is taken forward through the prioritisation of those deemed most in need for intervention.  Details of the planned list of carriageway and footway resurfacing schemes for the 2025/26 financial year will be included within my Department’s Annual Report to Councils.My officials will continue to undertake regular inspections and repair defects in accordance with the Department’s Limited Service policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what measures she is taking to improve road safety.
To ask the Minister of Finance for his assessment of the Spending Review 2025 settlement.
answer_text: The agreement I reached with the Treasury has resulted in an additional £600 million over the Spending Review period. This brings the total of additional provided funding provided through successful negotiations by my predecessor and I with the Treasury to £1.3bn for public services here. This means the cliff edge I have previously warned about has been avoided.However, the Spending Review itself was disappointing in that it did not provide the economic stimulus it could have. While in accountancy terms we have received additional Barnett funding of £3.1 billion in day-to-day funding over three years it must be viewed in the context of the many growing demands and the increased costs of delivering public services.The reality is the Executive will still remain in a financially constrained position particularly in 2026-27 with the majority of the funding not coming until future years.It is clear that significant challenges remain for our public services.While we will see more capital funding there was the potential to be more ambitious and spur economic growth.That said, we must now focus on the future. I will work together with my Ministerial colleagues to develop a multi-year budget – this will enable the Executive to plan on a longer-term basis.I have been clear that I want to create the conditions and circumstances for transformation and develop a multi-year budget that supports families, workers and communities.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update, including a timeframe, on the new build for St. Killian's College, Carnlough.
answer_text: The new build project for St Killian’s College is currently at the Spatial Co-ordination and Developed Design stage (RIBA Stage 3).Planning approval for the new school project was granted in May 2022, and approval for an additional block for sixth form and administration accommodation was granted in April 2025.Unfortunately following the conclusion of this current stage this Design and Build project will not be permitted to progress further at this time due to the current budget position.  As I have previously stated, it is my intention to continue to make the case for significant additional and sustained capital budget to invest right across the schools’ estate

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the progress of the new build for Mary Queen of Peace Primary School, Glenravel.
answer_text: The design of the new build project for Mary Queen of Peace Primary School has been completed and the Department’s technical advisors are currently reviewing pre-tender construction cost estimates. The procurement of a building contractor is in progress and it is estimated that construction of the new build school will commence on site in September/October 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) the preparatory work in advance of; and (ii) the next steps following the completion of, the Research for Better Health and Social Care Strategy (2016-25).
answer_text: In advance of completion of “Research for Better Health and Social Care Strategy (2016-25)”, there has been an evaluation of implementation of the strategy which is currently being finalised ahead of publication.  Further to this, there has been ongoing engagement with stakeholders in relation to the development of a follow up strategy, with the intention of launching this towards the end of 2025.   This has included a number of bilateral meetings, a workshop and discussion by the HSC R&D Division Strategic Advisory Board.   In addition, there has been discussion with representatives of UK Research and Innovation (UKRI) and the Health Research Board of Ireland (HRB).

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the availability of Produodopa for the treatment of Parkinson’s disease.
answer_text: My Department has a formal link with the National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE) under which NICE Technology Appraisals are reviewed locally for their legal and policy applicability here, and where found to be applicable, they are endorsed for implementation within Health and Social Care (HSC) organisations. In practice, this means that treatments that have been recommended by NICE for routine use in the NHS in England are also available in Northern Ireland.NICE guidance on the use of foslevodopa–foscarbidopa (Produodopa®) for treating advanced Parkinson’s with motor symptoms in adults, was endorsed by my Department in December 2023 and is available to eligible patients in Northern Ireland on a cost-per-case basis, in line with the current NICE guidelines (TA934), where infrastructure is in place and the service has capacity.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for a timeline on the progress of the legislation on the secondary use of data.
answer_text: The Health and Social Care (Control of Data Processing) Act (Northern Ireland) 2016 Act received Royal Ascent on 11th April 2016. Following unavoidable interruptions, planning resumed in 2022 for the introduction of the required regulations to enable the sharing of identifiable health data for secondary usages, beyond the limited and specific conditions available at present. The process to draft regulations included a full review of the Act (2016) with guidance from the Department Solicitors office. This review identified a substantive issue in the wording of the 2016 Act, regarding the scope of applicable data which could have encroached on the original intent of the legislation.My Department is currently progressing work to bring a "Bill of Amendment" to address the deficiencies identified.  Although I am seeking to introduce the Bill before the Summer recess, advancement is dependent on the NI Assembly legislative timetable and processes.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an assessment of the proportion of his budget allocated towards (i) prevention measures; (ii) early detection; and (iii) addressing health inequalities.
answer_text: Supporting people to improve their health and wellbeing, prevent ill-health, identify issues and address these at an early stage, and reduce health inequalities is central to the work of my Department and the wider Health and Social Care System. Given the nature of the work, it is very difficult to disaggregate exact budgets for prevention, early intervention and addressing inequalities, as these are often part of the core function of a range of services which cover prevention early intervention, treatment and support and longer-term recovery and tertiary prevention. The Public Health Agency has a statutory role to improve health and reduce health inequalities. Prevention and early intervention are key priorities in this area. The Agency works with a range of partners in HSC, the community and voluntary sector and across the public sector to deliver this work. Making Life Better, the Executive’s overarching strategic framework for public health, therefore has a key indicator on the spend on public health as measured by the Public Health Agency’s resource outturn. While this is only one element of the spend in these areas, the latest published figure for this expenditure was £134.5m in 2022/23. This includes health protection, health improvement, screening, and vaccinations. Across all these areas there is likely to be additional investment via SPPG and the Health and Social Care Trusts.Research has shown that only 20% of health outcomes are driven by the clinical care we receive, 40% are related to the socio-economic environment in which people live, 10% are related to the physical environment, and the final 30% are the result of a range of behaviours. Therefore, it is important to note that improving health and wellbeing and addressing health inequalities requires input across government to tackle issues such as poverty, social exclusion, educational attainment, etc.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the Cancer Strategy 2022-2032, including (i) progress made to date; (ii) priorities for the next two years; and (iii) funding allocated for implementation.
answer_text: The Cancer Strategy was published in March 2022, with a recurrent investment of £10.6m in 2024/25 to allow for strategy implementation. I recognise that that this falls short of £34.2m outlined in the Cancer Strategy Funding Plan published alongside the Cancer Strategy, however, this reflects the financial challenges and competing pressures right across the Health and Social Care system.Despite these financial challenges, good progress has been made on a range of priority projects, including:The establishment of 2 Rapid Diagnosis Centres (RDCs) in Whiteabbey and South Tyrone hospitals in December. Since July 2024 the Vague Symptom Pathway is a fully regional service, with patients in all five Trusts able to access RDC’s through their GP.A demand and capacity model for Systemic Anti Cancer Therapy (SACT) has been completed.Optimal pathways reviews for the top three haematology pathways (3 main blood cancers, Myeloma, Lymphoma and Acute) have been completed. These reviews will now form the basis for a service transformation project.A review of Adolescent and Young Adult Cancer Services has been completed and minimum standards for the service have been developed. A new regionally networked service model is in the process of being established. This will ensure that patients have equitable access to services, with a regional multi-disciplinary meeting discussing the care and psychosocial needs of all AYA patients.  A review of Advanced Communication Skills training has been completed.  A total of 132 staff received training in 2023/24, with a further 177 staff received training in 2024/25. In addition, the number of training facilitators is being expanded.Northern Ireland was the first part of the UK to commit to implement the Pancreatic Cancer Optimal Care pathway. It’s being used as a test site for future work on other tumour sites.A review of Breast Cancer services is also underway and a regional waiting list for breast cancer patients launched in May 2025. My Departmental officials are currently finalising a Cancer Research Strategy that will support cancer clinical trials and research that can help to deliver improved patient outcomes.A strategy Implementation Plan 2025-28 has been developed for the next 3 years and this will ensure that the priorities for further progression and investment in cancer services are clearly identified. It is however important to recognise that the available budget poses significant challenges and directly impacts what can be achieved.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the current level of care available for those living with Paediatric Autoimmune Neuropsychiatric Disorders Associated with Streptococcal Infections.
answer_text: I would first of all say that I recognise that this condition causes distress and concern for children and their families.  Although rare there has been increasing occurrences of presentations of Paediatric Autoimmune Neuropsychiatric Disorders Associated (PANDA) to paediatric services and Paediatric Acute-onset Neuropsychiatric Syndrome (PANS) associated with streptococcal infections.Regarding any potential barriers within the HSC, treatment options will vary dependent on the clinical presentation and paediatricians would welcome more information to treat patients more effectively when this becomes available from national partners.  Plans are in place regionally to create a clinically led network that allows clinicians to share best practice and gain more understanding of new and emerging issues.  A regional steering group for all paediatric services has been created and a Clinical Advisory Group chaired by a paediatrician is planned to commence in the next few months.  It is hoped that conditions such as PANS/PANDA will be discussed within this group. However, with such a wide and varied range of paediatric issues/conditions to be considered and limited clinical capacity for clinical discussion, prioritisation will be required.Regarding individual care where a presentation occurs, a child will normally be seen at their local District General Hospital by a paediatrician who will assess the child as there are usually a number of symptoms related to this disorder.  The paediatrician will review each presentation and based on this may request additional support from the regional neurological service in Belfast Health and Social Care Trust (BHSCT) or from their local Child and Adolescent Mental Health Service (CAMHS), amongst others in order to put in place a treatment plan.It must be noted, as there are also potentially neuropsychiatric implications with this disorder, there will be a need to engage with CAMHS colleagues in Trusts as they also have a crucial role to play in care and treatment of children with this condition.We must recognise that there are very significant demands on CAMHS services as well as other paediatric services across the HSC, which are impacting on increased waiting times and my department are working with Trusts to identify improvements.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to provide an update on the pay award for further education lecturers.

To ask the Minister of Health for his assessment of the two Rapid Diagnostic Centres, including details of any plans to expand (i) the number of centres; and (ii) the range of diagnostic tests provided for within the centres.
answer_text: The two Rapid Diagnostic Centres (RDCs) located at South Tyrone Hospital and Whiteabbey Hospital continue to deliver a regional service for patients with vague but concerning symptoms that could indicate cancer.  The RDCs are currently operating at the full commissioned capacity for the Vague Symptom Pathway (VSP) service. Patients receive a same-day, face-to-face clinical assessment and the diagnostic tests they require, greatly reducing the time it takes to make a radiological diagnosis of cancer and for the patient to commence treatment.Capital funding was secured for one additional CT and MRI scanner linked to each RDC site. Capacity that is not being used to support the VSP is being used to address regional red flag/urgent waiting lists for CT and MRI.Clinical Reference Groups are currently considering how the RDC model may be utilised to improve red flag cancer pathways, with a particular focus on high volume tumour sites. The delivery of new diagnostic pathways through RDCs may require additional diagnostic procedures to be provided from the two existing RDCs, or for additional RDC sites to be identified and established.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any plans he has for the expansion of medical associate professions, including anaesthesia associates.
answer_text: My Department is currently undertaking a general evaluation of the Physician Associate role across the HSC which is intended will provide a firm evidence base for the future of this role in Northern Ireland.My Department also considers any recommendations regarding the Physician Associate profession emerging through long term, strategic workforce planning.  The Medical Workforce Plan for Psychiatric Specialties 2022 – 2031, published by my Department in July 2024, recommends that HSC Trusts, ideally collectively, should determine the need for, and explore the feasibility of, recruiting Physician Associates to support medical staff in mental health.  There are a small number of Anaesthesia Associates employed by HSC trusts. The Department currently does not have any plans to expand this profession.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he plans to increase the number of medical speciality training places (i) in total; and (ii) within anaesthesia.
answer_text: On the 14 May 2025 I announced a significant package of investment in Northern Ireland’s Health and Social Care workforce. The expansion includes 24 new medical specialty training posts in programmes delivered on my Department’s behalf by the Northern Ireland Medical and Dental Training Agency. 13 of these places are in Internal Medicine, 5 in Core Psychiatry, and 6 in other medical specialties.You will appreciate that there are many priorities to consider, however I can advise that an expansion of Anaesthetics training places is very much in the mix under consideration.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to provide a breakdown of funding invested into speciality training places in the 2024-25 financial year.

To ask the Minister for Communities to confirm that the Gambling Code of Practice, when published, will stipulate that no person should be permitted to withdraw cash from a financial institution, by ATM or any other method, within a premises for which bingo and gaming for prizes is authorised by a bingo club licence under The Betting, Gaming, Lotteries and Amusements (Northern Ireland) Order 1985.
answer_text: I refer the Member to the answer to question AQW 25650/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any plans her Department has to upgrade the road surface of the Cushendall Road between Ballycastle and Cushendall, including an associated time frame.
answer_text: My Department has been operating in a difficult financial environment for a number of years due to underfunding and austerity by the British Government.While there are many roads that would benefit from investment, due to limited budget resources, resurfacing work is taken forward through the prioritisation of those deemed most in need for intervention.Details of the resurfacing schemes planned for the 2025/26 financial year will be included in my Department’s Annual Report to Council.My officials will continue to undertake regular inspections and repair defects in accordance with the Department’s Limited Service policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether his Department has considered research published by the Gambling Commission on 6 February 2025 that suggests gaming machines are associated with a higher risk of experiencing problem gambling.
answer_text: My Department is aware of, and is considering, research published by the Gambling Commission on 6 February 2025 entitled “Exploring the relationship between gambling activities and Problem Gambling Severity Index (PGSI) scores”, which surveyed respondents in Great Britain.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the statutory limit on the number of gaming machines which may be used in licensed bingo clubs.
answer_text: There is no statutory limit on the number of gaming machines which may be used in licensed bingo clubs. A licensing court has power to specify a maximum limit when considering an application for the grant or renewal of a bingo club licence.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail (i) the number of licenced bingo clubs in the North; and (ii) the average number of gaming machines operated per licenced bingo club in the North.
answer_text: Latest available official statistics for 2023 show there were 37 bingo club licences operational in Northern Ireland under The Betting, Gaming, Lotteries and Amusements (NI) Order 1985. The Department does not hold data on the average number of gaming machines operated per licensed bingo club in Northern Ireland.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether Forestry service can provide (i) the spatial information they have stated in relation to their sites; and (ii) evidence of the species that have been spreading within their estates that have been removed in the last five years.
answer_text: I can confirm that Forest Service hold the spatial data referred to in AQW 23098/22-27 on its Geographical Information System. This information is available to share with other parties through an appropriate data sharing agreement, in line with Government data sharing requirements. Requests for sharing spatial forestry data can be directed to Forest Service GIS team on forestservicegis@daera-ni.gov.uk. The information available spatially, includes details of the species targeted and areas treated across the forest estate, primarily laurel, rhododendron, Pheasant berry, Japanese knotweed and Giant Hogweed, in each of the last 5 years.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when will repair works at the Glenshesk Road, Ballycastle, be completed.

To ask the Minister for Communities for his assessment of whether there is a disproportionate impact for local claimants as a result of the British Government's announcement on changes to the benefits system.

To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether Forestry Service has a specific action for the removal of species, specifically (i) rhondondendron ponticum; (ii) cherry laurel (prunus laurocerasus); and (iii) pheasant berry (leycesteria formosa), given they are damaging to both biodiversity and productive forestry.
answer_text: Forest Service take specific action to control rhododendron and laurel species where their presence threatens bio-diversity, and in particular in designated areas.A control programme specifically for rhododendron and laurel species has treated 220 hectares over the past 5 years.  I understand that, based on observation by Forest Service staff, the presence of pheasant berry is not a common across the forest estate and no specific action plan is in place for its control.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, pursuant to AQW 15916/22-27, on what information, surveys, records or otherwise the conclusion that complete removal of invasive species from the forest estate is not practical nor achievable was reached.
answer_text: In my response to AQW 15916/22-27, my reference to it not being not practical nor achievable, to completely remove many invasive species from the forest estate, is due to many species already considered to be present in the wider environment.  Forest Service holds specific spatial information on the presence of invasive plant species which required the area to be treated due to risks posed to designated areas, the sustainable management of the forest and in particular, where there is a threat to ancient woodlands.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on an Irish language strategy.
answer_text: The Irish Language Strategy, alongside the Ulster-Scots Language, Heritage and Culture Strategy, is being developed through a co-design process, in which my department is taking a lead on behalf of the Executive. 		A Cross Departmental Working Group of senior officials nominated by Ministers has been established and is considering work to date.		This work is intended to provide a strategy and action plan that can be provided to the Executive for consideration. Public consultation will follow Executive consideration and approval of the drafts. 	The detailed timing for this work is to a large degree outside the Department’s control, recognising the range of work priorities across Departments. 		Alongside the work of this Cross-Departmental Working Group I have been meeting with officials to consider the next steps in the development of both language strategies. I anticipate that the next stage will be for me to bring a paper to the Executive to ask them to consider and, if content, agree on next steps.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether she will take measures to increase her Department's enforcement of unadopted developments.
To ask the Minister of Finance for an update on the funding required by departments as a result of Storm Éowyn.
To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the Electricity Connection Policy Framework.
answer_text: Question 9 – AQO 1518/22-27My Department published a consultation on the Electricity Connection Policy Framework on 30 January, which closes on 24 April.  My preferred option is for full socialisation of the reinforcement costs for new network connections to the distribution electricity network.  This will make it easier for people to connect to the grid, promote regional balance, and help the drive towards net zero. Following the consultation, my department will publish a decision paper in the second quarter of 2025 and we will engage with the Utility Regulator and NIE Networks with the aim of implementation by the end of this year.The proposals in this consultation paper will bring the North more into line with connection policy in the South and in Britain.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the implementation of the Identity and Language (Northern Ireland) Act 2022.
answer_text: The appointment of the Language Commissioners and the Members of the Office of Identity and Cultural Expression is essential to implement the provisions of the Identity and Language (Northern Ireland) Act 2022, for which TEO is responsible. We are committed to establishing the three new bodies, and preparations are now at an advanced stage. We hope to be in a position to formally launch the competitions shortly.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail any actions she is taking to prevent retail crime.
answer_text: I refer the member to AQW 20265/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice when the Sentencing Bill will be introduced to the Assembly.
answer_text: As you will be aware, it was not possible to introduce any legislation or progress key policy decisions in the absence of Ministers, the Executive and NI Assembly during a significant proportion of the early part of this mandate. Following revisions to my planned legislative programme to allow for this significantly shortened mandate, I secured Executive agreement to commence drafting the Sentencing Bill on 19th December and I aim to introduce it in the autumn of this year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to outline what impact an increase in employers' National Insurance contributions will have on public sector employers.
answer_text: The planned increase to Employers’ National Insurance Contributions will prove incredibly challenging for our public sector employers.Departments estimate the additional cost to departments, their agencies, Non-Departmental Public Bodies and Arm’s Length Bodies arising from the increase in National Insurance Contributions to be around £200 million for 2025/26.Furthermore, this doesn’t include the costs facing healthcare providers; local councils; or for the community and voluntary sectors. For example, the Department of Health has estimated an additional annual cost of some £36.5m for Family Health Service providers and independent providers.I also understand the Department for Communities has received information from local government which estimates an additional cost to Councils of around £12m.While the Chancellor has said that support will be provided for departments and public sector employers, and we are waiting on confirmation on the funding the Executive will receive, our view is that this is likely to fall far short, possibly by some £100 million pounds. This was a decision taken in Westminster on a reserved taxation measure and the Executive does not have the ability to mitigate against that.I have written to the Chief Secretary to the Treasury pressing for urgent clarity on the quantum of funding we will receive and asking for these costs to be met in full. I have also appealed for consideration to be given to support for those outside central government who provide vital public services.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, given the projected increase in inheritance tax liabilities for farmers due to changes in Agricultural Property Relief from April 2026, what steps his Department is taking to support farmers.
answer_text: I have been very clear in my conversations with the UK government that the changes announced in the Budget in respect of agricultural property relief and business property relief have caused great concern in the agricultural community and will have significant adverse impacts for farming families in Northern Ireland.Within my remit as DAERA Minister, I will do all I can to continue to support the farming community in Northern Ireland on this issue. However, as taxation it is not a devolved matter, this does place limitations on the measures that I can take. Therefore, I must be clear, that it will not be possible for me to mitigate against the impact of these tax relief changes by making a payment to refund part or all of an inheritance tax bill.There are actions that farmers can take to reduce future tax liability but these will depend on individual circumstances and farmers are encouraged to seek tax advice from an appropriate professional before making any decisions.My Department’s new Farm Support and Development Programme contains a Farming for the Generations Scheme which aims to raise awareness of the need for succession planning on farms supporting farm families through a three phased approach: planning for succession, developing the successor, and supporting the lead generation within the farm family. The scheme will also link farmers without a family successor to new entrants to provide access to land and other resources.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to provide an update on the appointment of Commissioners under the Identity and Language (Northern Ireland) Act 2022.

To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail any consideration he has given to the introduction of a form of licencing of activity involving animals, which would allow for the regulation of animal rehoming organisations.
answer_text: I intend to bring forward legislation on a range of important animal welfare issues and have invited the Northern Ireland Companion Animal Welfare Group to meet with me on 4 December to discuss priority reforms. As part of these discussions, I wish to explore the merits of introducing new licensing and regulatory requirements for the rescue and rehoming sector.However, I am mindful of what can be delivered within the time remaining in this mandate and I want to ensure the best use of resources to address the most pressing issues.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 5449/22-27, for an update on the proposed new footpath from Portglenone village, along Gortgole Road towards Bracknamuckley Forest and Portglenone Gaelic Athletic Club grounds.
answer_text: I can confirm that my Department is proposing to provide a footway from Portglenone village, along Gortgole Road towards Bracknamuckley Forest and Portglenone Gaelic Athletic Club grounds, finishing at Lovers Lane. This scheme is currently at design stage. Whilst this scheme is programmed for 2025/26 it is subject to design being completed, land aquisition, and the availability of funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the measures his Department has taken to keep local residents of Glenshesk Road, Ballycastle, informed of the progress of work being carried out by his Department with associated time frames.
answer_text: In July 2024, My Department met with elected officials, representing the Glens area, to provide an update on progress to date.  In August 2024, a letter was issued to residents living within the vicinity of the Glenshesk Road closure by way of an update.It is now my Department’s intention to publish relevant information online for the benefit of the public and to provide meaningful updates in relation to progress as the detailed design develops and construction is scheduled.  An update will also be provided in relation to the recent slippage on the Glenshesk Road due to Storm Bert, which I am happy to advise is currently under repair.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on progress of the work being carried out to ensure Glenshesk Road, Ballycastle, at the location of the landslide in March 2024, is reopened to traffic as quickly as possible.
answer_text: My Department, and their consultants, are currently developing the detailed design solution for the embankment failure at Glenshesk Road.  It is anticipated the design process for the remedial works will be completed in early 2025, with construction works implemented immediately after in spring, subject to funding and favourable weather conditions.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether the Review of the Northern Ireland Curriculum will consider a statutory requirement for schools to educate pupils on the dangers of gambling as part of the core curriculum.
answer_text: I would refer the Member to the answer to question AQW 17640/22-27.   The answer is contained in the Written Answers Booklet for week ending 22 November 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail responsibility for dealing with Japanese knotweed on private land, to ensure it does not spread to neighbouring properties.
answer_text: It is not an offence to have Japanese knotweed growing on private property, neither is there any statutory mechanism in Northern Ireland to compel a landowner/manager to control Japanese knotweed, or to permit others to carry out control measures without an owner or occupier’s consent. Where Japanese knotweed is spreading from one property to another, it constitutes a civil matter between the two respective landowners and must be resolved as any other issue of neighbouring property maintenance would be.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for his assessment of the Go Succeed service.

To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any representations he has made to the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport in relation to receiving a share of the proposed statutory levy on remote gambling operators licenced by the Gambling Commission.
answer_text: I have written, alongside the Ministers of Health and Education, to the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, The Rt Hon. Lisa Nandy MP, seeking early engagement on the possibility of Northern Ireland receiving a share of the proposed statutory levy on remote gambling operators licensed by the Gambling Commission. answer_text: I refer the Member to the answer to question AQW 16313/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any representations he has made to the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport in relation to receiving a share of the proposed statutory levy on remote gambling operators licenced by the Gambling Commission.
answer_text: On September 17th, I, together with the Minister of Health and the Minister for Communities, communicated with the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport through a letter. The purpose of this communication was to explore the opportunity for Northern Ireland to receive an appropriate share of the statutory levy on gambling operators, as outlined in the United Kingdom Government White Paper, ‘High stakes: gambling reform for the digital age,’ which was published on April 27, 2023. The Minister responsible for gambling at the Department for Culture, Media and Sport, Baroness Twycross, has directed there should be official engagement on this issue.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any representations he has made to the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport in relation to receiving a share of the proposed statutory levy on remote gambling operators licenced by the Gambling Commission.
answer_text: On 17 September, I, along with the Ministers for Communities and Education, wrote to the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, The Rt Hon. Lisa Nandy MP, seeking early engagement on the possibility of Northern Ireland receiving a share of the GB statutory levy on gambling operators,  proposed in the UK Government White Paper, ‘High stakes: gambling reform for the digital age’, and published on 27 April 2023.  A response to this letter has been received from Baroness Twycross, the Minister for Gambling, stating that in the early stages of the new government they are still considering the full range of gambling policy, recognising the shared interests and concerns regarding the reduction of gambling harm. A commitment was given that as part of this consideration, appropriate engagement will take place, including that with Ministers in Northern Ireland, as necessary. Officials will engage with UK government officials to follow up on the concerns raised regarding the statutory levy.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to provide an update on the appointment of Commissioners under the Identity and Language (Northern Ireland) Act 2022.
answer_text: The appointment of the Commissioners, the Director and other Members of the Office of Identity and Cultural Expression is essential to implementing the provisions of the Identity and Language (NI) Act 2022, for which TEO is responsible.We are committed to establishing the three new bodies, and preparations are now at an advanced stage. question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether Forest Service plans to further develop and take responsibility for the multiple community benefit of woodlands for access and biodiversity as a properly planned community and tourism asset.
answer_text: Forest Service has for some years been working to develop and promote forests to increase the recreational and tourism benefits they provide for visitors, local businesses and the wider economy. Well-established and successful partnership arrangements are in place with local councils and recreational partners, which have enabled significant funding and investment in forest based recreational infrastructure.  I am keen for this work to continue and enable forests to be developed for access that brings greater benefits to local communities, and supports wider tourism initiatives, whilst ensuring that our biodiversity is protected.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, given that invasive plants threaten long-term forestry production, biodiversity and are a source of the spread of these species into the wider environment, whether Forest Service plans to remove them from their woodlands.
answer_text: The Forest Service forests are managed to meet internationally recognised sustainable forestry management standards.  This is certified through independent professional annual audit that the forest management complies with these standards. Environmental protection is a core aspect of the standards for sustainable forestry management and includes requirements for the control of invasive species. Forest Service operates an on-going work programme for the treatment of invasive species, which focuses on environmental protection of higher value conservation. Many invasive species are already classified as widespread within the wider environment and spread freely from both the forest estate and adjacent land. Complete removal of invasive species from the forest estate is not practical nor achievable.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the public competition to recruit a new Victims Commissioner.
To ask the Minister of Health to outline what steps his Department is taking to provide respite services for people who need them.
answer_text: Regionally respite/short break availability for young people with disability is a current pressure across Trusts. There has been an increase in children & young people reported to have disability and there has been an increasing demand for access to short breaks & respite services. Some Children / Young People with a disability have also required longer-term residential placements, and this has led to occasions when overnight Short-Break (Respite Care) units have had children / young people placed there for longer than 90 days. This has meant that these units at times cannot maintain a Short Break ServiceThere can be challenges accessing short break/respite beds in Adult Learning Disability Services, with demand sometimes outstripping supply. On occasion short break/respite beds may be used to facilitate an emergency admission to support an individual, their carer or family during a crisis. Whilst it is extremely important to support a family during such a crisis, emergency admissions can negatively impact on others accessing planned or pre-booked short break/respite beds within a facility for a period. DoH officials are working to finalise ‘The Learning Disability Service Model’ which will set out a new model for the future of Learning Disability Services, through the lens of regional consistency and supporting needs at an early stage in the community. An anticipated outcome of the service model is that families and carers will be offered the flexible, practical and emotional support that they need to support adults with a learning disability to live in the community and have a meaningful life. It is envisaged that this will involve increasing the level and diversity of short breaks/respite services, including consideration to modernise existing services and environments to better support those with more complex needs. Engagement with individuals with a learning disability, families/carers will remain central to finalisation of the service model.Following COVID, Trusts have reported that the complexity of their service users who avail of short breaks has increased.  Care homes beds may also not be ring-fenced for short breaks due to the requirement to support hospital discharge.  Trusts are therefore reliant on bed availability at the point of assessment of need.  There has been a 1.6% reduction in the number of care home beds since March 2020, with the closure of 15 care homes and a reduction of 257 beds. This reduces access to traditional bed-based provision. All requests for short breaks are assessed and managed by the HSC Trusts based on individual need, with statutory and independent sector care homes facilitating both planned and emergency short breaks.As is the case for older people, adults with a physical disability requesting short breaks are assessed and managed by the HSC Trust based on individual need.  There is less capacity within the care home sector to support short breaks provision due to the pressures to facilitate hosp

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the number of schools which have applied to establish specialist provision.
answer_text: There is no application process for the establishment of specialist provision in mainstream schools.  The Education Authority engages with schools in areas where there is an identified need for specialist provision.To support the Education Authority in creating additional specialist provision across the education sector to meet the significant need for the 2024/25 academic year, my department wrote to all schools in April 2024 seeking their interest in establishing specialist provision.  There was a very positive response with over 400 schools indicating a willingness to establish new or additional specialist provision classes.  Joint engagement with my department and the Education Authority took place in May and June 2024 with schools in pressure areas which said they could establish provision by September 2024 and a number of these have resulted in the establishment of new specialist provision for this academic year.Further engagement with the remainder of the schools who responded positively to the survey is planned to take place this school term in preparation for September 2025 and beyond.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to detail his priorities in relation to improved animal welfare legislation for pets during this Assembly mandate.
answer_text: I intend to bring forward legislation on a range of important animal welfare issues including a number of issues directly affecting the welfare of pets during this mandate, but I am also mindful of what can be delivered within the resources available to my Department and the time remaining in this mandate.		In the immediate future, I have asked that my officials progress work on the introduction of Lucys Law in Northern Ireland and provide options for my consideration on the mandatory use of CCTV in abattoirs. This is in addition to ongoing work to introduce XL Bully type dog safeguarding measures and implementing a specific offence for pet abduction.		I am very conscious of stakeholder views and I wish to restate my commitment to engaging with these organisations in the time ahead, as I determine the direction for animal welfare policy and legislation.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the roll-out of multi-disciplinary teams across GP services in North Antrim.
answer_text: The Multi-Disciplinary Team (MDT) model remains the key to stabilising and strengthening vital Primary Care services to ensure they can continue to provide high quality care to our people, now and into the future.  I want to be able to further invest in the Primary Care MDTs, so more people can benefit from prompt access to a range of support across primary care.However, while the budget for 2024/25 has been set it is expected that the overall financial position for all Northern Ireland (NI) departments will remain very challenging. My Department is developing a plan for rollout to all remaining GP Federation areas, including North Antrim, over the next number of years subject to the availability of the necessary funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for an update and timeframe for the introduction of the Small Woodland Grant Scheme for 2024.
answer_text: My Department is currently taking forward work to secure the necessary approvals to fund 2024 applications for forestry grant schemes, including for the Small Woodland Grant Scheme. Forest Service is leading a review of application periods for these schemes, with input from relevant stakeholders. I anticipate that schemes will be available for applications by early autumn, at which time they will be promoted by the Department on its website and via social media.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to outline when the consultation will commence with victims and survivors of mother and baby homes on the establishment of an inquiry and a financial redress scheme.
To ask the Minister for Communities whether his Department has any plans to carry out a public realm scheme in Portglenone.
answer_text: My Department has a regeneration policy focus on urban settlements with a population of 5,000 or more people. As Portglenone has a population below the 5,000 threshold, it will not be possible for my Department to provide funding for a Public Realm and Environmental Improvement project at this time.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether the eligibility criteria for the Energy Efficiency Capital Grant will be kept under review.
To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on Programme for Government preparations.
answer_text: We are moving forward quickly with work to agree and publish a draft Programme for Government which not only reflects the need to significantly transform our public services, but also sets out our collective priorities for this mandate.

To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the rural needs testing for 2023- 24 to identify hidden housing need in (i) Dunloy; and (ii) Rasharkin.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Housing Executive.  I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows.“A Housing Need Test was undertaken for Dunloy in 2021/22. Following the test, the Housing Executive are in the process of completing a development brief for the transfer of Housing Executive lands at Station Road to Clanmil Housing to develop 11 social housing units.  This scheme has been added to the Social Housing Development Programme (SHDP) and is due to start onsite in 2024/25.A Housing Need Test was undertaken for Rasharkin in 2021/22, which resulted in the Housing Executive supporting a transfer site at Church Road for 10 units in the first phase, by Triangle Housing.  This scheme is on the SHDP and is due to start onsite in 2024/25.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Northern Ireland Housing Executive directly. Making direct contact with the Northern Ireland Housing Executive will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the development of the proposed new children's hospital at the RVH site.
answer_text: The Full Business Case for the Belfast Trust’s New Children’s Hospital is currently being reviewed by the Department of Health and officials continue to work with the Trust to advance the project to contract award stage.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) the work required to repair Glenshesk Road, Ballycastle, at the site of the landslide; and (ii) a timeframe for the completion of this work.
answer_text: The Glenshesk Road, Ballycastle has been closed between the junctions of Glenbank Road and Greenan Road, since 27 March 2024 due to a partial road collapse resulting from a landslide after a period of prolonged heavy rain.My Department has arranged for a detailed geotechnical investigation to fully assess the extent of the damage.  When this work is complete, my officials will be able to progress the design and, subject to the necessary approvals, construction of remedial works.   As this is a complex issue is likely to take some time to complete the investigation and develop and implement an appropriate solution. As such, it is not currently possible to advise when the road will reopen.My Department will continue to work at pace to reopen this road as quickly as possible and will keep the public informed of any updates.
